checks.a_analysis: The output document's content is consistently derived from the source PDF. The agenda items, meeting minutes from March 10, 2022, and the various technology committee updates (ESINet, GIS, NMAC) all match the provided OCR and source images. There are no invented paragraphs or fabricated sections.

All URLs identified in the output, specifically the 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)', match the source document (page 7). No phone numbers or emails were present in either the source or the conversion.

The conversion covers the entirety of the 8-page document. It includes the agenda for the June 9 meeting, the minutes of the March 10 meeting, and the detailed status reports on various board projects located at the end of the document.

Three major tables in the 'Minutes' section are severely truncated. The 'Attendance' table (source page 2) contains 26 data rows for members and staff, but the output only contains 2 rows (Rachel Bello, Randy Cress). The 'Guests' table (source pages 2-3) contains 10 individuals, but the output only lists 2. The 'Minutes Vote' table (source page 3) contains 11 rows, whereas the output lists only 2. In each instance, over 80% of the row data is missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table on page 2 contains 26 data rows (11 Members, 15 Staff). Guests table on pages 2-3 contains 10 rows. Minutes Vote table on page 3 contains 11 rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: Attendance table (index under heading-3) contains only 2 data rows (Bello, Cress). Guests table contains only 2 rows (Briggs, Eaton). Minutes Vote table (index under heading-4) contains only 2 rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While the tables mentioned in C4 are truncated, the narrative portions of the document, such as the descriptions of the ESINet, NMAC, and GIS updates, are complete and reflect the full content of the source.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output maintains high fidelity to the source text. It even correctly replicates inconsistencies present in the original document, such as the variant spellings of 'Jeff Ship' and 'Jeff Shipp' across different pages.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output follows the source document's repetition of headers (Meeting details) and footers (Next Meeting date) which appear on both the agenda page and the subsequent minutes/materials pages. No erroneous duplication of paragraphs, sections, or list items within their respective segments was identified.

The output maintains a logical heading hierarchy. H2 is used for major document titles and section headers throughout, with H3 correctly used for subsections such as 'Minutes Vote' under the 'MINUTES' section and 'Next Up Migrations' under the 'ESINet Update' section. No levels are skipped, and the nesting reflects the source's organization.

The structural fidelity of several tables is severely compromised due to truncation. The 'Attendance' table (spanning pages 2-3 in the source with approximately 30 rows) only contains 2 data rows in the output. Similarly, the 'Guests' table (page 3, 10 entries) and the 'Minutes Vote' table (page 3, 11 rows) are truncated to only 2 rows each. This represents a major failure in preserving the table's structural organization and content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table (Pages 2-3) containing rows for Rachel Bello through Evan Warner and several staff; Guests list (Page 3) containing 10 entries; Minutes Vote table (Page 3) containing 11 rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 6 (Attendance table) truncated to 2 rows (Rachel Bello, Randy Cress); Element 7 (Guests table) truncated to 2 rows; Element 10 (Minutes Vote table) truncated to 2 rows.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output faithfully follows the flow of the source document, progressing from the agenda to the minutes and then through the meeting slides/materials in the order they appear in the PDF.

Lists are correctly identified and formatted. The multi-level agenda on Page 1 is properly represented as a nested list. The numbered items in the minutes and the bulleted project notes on later pages are accurately captured as list structures.

The output fails to preserve functional hyperlinks present in the source document. While the text for URLs (e.g., 'arcgis.com') and linked page titles is present, they are rendered as plain text without the underlying link metadata or being wrapped in a clickable element.
checks.b_evidence_source: Clickable links on Page 7: 'NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage' and 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)'
checks.b_evidence_output: Plain text strings for these items without functional links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document generally follows the visual organization of the source PDF. It maintains the single-column flow and the sequence of sections (Agenda, then Minutes, then current meeting details). Normal HTML adaptations like the addition of a Table of Contents and different font styling are present but do not disrupt the visual organization of information.

Several tables in the converted HTML are severely truncated and missing most of their content. Specifically, the 'Attendance' tables for 'Members', 'Staff', and 'Guests' (pages 2-3 of the PDF) are missing nearly all rows. The 'Minutes Vote' table (page 3) is also truncated, showing only two entries out of eleven. This makes the data in these sections unreadable and incomplete.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF shows complete tables for Members (11 names), Staff (15 names), Guests (9 entries), and Minutes Vote (11 names) across pages 2 and 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the 'Members' table with only 2 rows, the 'Staff' table is entirely missing, the 'Guests' table has only 2 rows, and the 'Minutes Vote' table has only 2 rows.
checks.b_evidence_location: Minutes section, Attendance and Minutes Vote tables (corresponds to PDF pages 2-3)

checks.a_analysis: A significant data visualization, the 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' bar chart on page 6 of the PDF, is entirely missing from the HTML output. While the data table it represents is present on the previous page, the chart itself is a meaningful visual element of the source document that has not been rendered.
checks.b_evidence_source: Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet bar chart graphic
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML content jumps from the 'Current Deployment Service Types' table to the text '*Hosted Viper #49 is Macon Count', omitting the chart graphic entirely.
checks.b_evidence_location: After the 'Current Deployment Service Types' table (corresponds to PDF page 6)

checks.a_analysis: No visual content blocks (paragraphs, tables, or sections) appear to be duplicated in the rendered HTML output. The repetition of terms like 'Roll Call' and 'Approval of Minutes' reflects their dual appearance in the source PDF (once in the agenda, and once in the meeting details).
